title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Oracle WebLogic JMS sink
problem: OCI GoldenGate lists Oracle WebLogic JMS as a Big Data connection. nats-sinks does not yet define how events should be written into Oracle WebLogic JMS or how that differs from a future generic JMS sink.
proposal: Research a first-party Oracle WebLogic JMS sink profile and a relationship to a generic JMS sink. The design should cover JNDI configuration, TLS, authentication, message properties, payload envelope format, idempotency limitations, persistent delivery mode, transactions, acknowledgements, and durable handoff semantics.
users: Oracle WebLogic operators, integration teams, and application owners who need NATS JetStream events delivered into existing JMS estates.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires persistent JMS delivery and a documented transaction or send-commit boundary. Non-persistent or best-effort JMS modes must be disabled by default or clearly marked unsafe for durable processing.
security: Credentials, JNDI endpoints, queue names, and payloads must be redacted. TLS verification must be enabled by default. Queue and topic names must be allow-listed and bounded.
acceptance: Research identifies supported Python/JMS bridge options and their operational cost.
The design documents durable send semantics and duplicate-handling limits.
Persistent delivery is the secure default.
Unit tests use fakes and do not require WebLogic.
Documentation explains the relationship with a future generic JMS connector.
tests: Add fake JMS client contract tests, config validation tests, and optional integration guidance for a controlled WebLogic environment.
documentation: Add Oracle WebLogic JMS sink research documentation and update sink framework and roadmap pages.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
